700&=7 \times 100\\\\
7{,}000&=7 \times 1{,}000
\end{align}$

[[☃ interaction 3]]

*The $9$ moved to the thousands place. So, we need a $0$ in the ones, tens, and hundreds places.*

###The answer: 

$\begin{align}
90&=9 \times 10\\\\
900&=9 \times 100\\\\
9{,}000&=9 \times 1{,}000
\end{align}$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$6\,\vert$ | | |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $6$
$600\,\vert$ |  |  | $6$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $6$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $6$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$6 \blueD{\times 10 \times 10} =600$
$6 \blueD{\times100} = 600$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$7\,\vert$ | | | | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $7$
$70\,\vert$ |  |  |  | $7$ | $0$

The digits in $7$ moved $1$ place to the left. So we multiplied $7$ by $1$ factor of $10$.
$70= 7 \blueD{\times10}$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$3\,\vert$ | | |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $3$
$300\,\vert$ |  |  | $3$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $3$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $3$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\blueD{10 \times 10 \times} 3 =300$
$\blueD{100 \times} 3= 300$
Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place value to the left**.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$8\,\vert$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$| |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $8$
$8{,}000\,\vert$ | $8$ | $,$ | $0$ | $0$ | $0$

The digits in $8$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $8$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$8{,}000 = \blueD{10 \times 10 \times 10 \times} 8$
